title: T-049 — Shared BOM SSOT skeleton contract and package boundary
prompt:
# T-049 — Shared BOM SSOT skeleton contract and package boundary

PRD: `docs/prd/00-FOUNDATION-PRD.md` — sections: §W0-v4.3, §10
Project root: `/Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira`
ACP import shape: top-level TaskCreate only (`title`, `prompt`, `labels`, `priority`, `max_attempts`, `pipeline_name`, `pipeline_inputs`). Do not add generated ACP fields such as task_id/status/project_id. `pipeline_inputs.root_path` is mandatory.

## Goal
Create the foundation-level Shared BOM single-source-of-truth skeleton contract consumed by NPD, Technical, Planning, D365 adapter, and downstream modules.

## Locked Wave 0 decisions to honor
- Business data-plane scope is `org_id`; do not create new business-table `tenant_id` columns. Tenant/control-plane concepts may exist above org scope, but app/business rows, RLS policies, audit/outbox rows, role grants, and R13 skeletons use `org_id`.
- Canonical finished-good domain/event prefix is `fg.*`. `fa.*` / `FA` may appear only as an explicit legacy compatibility alias during migration, never as the primary new contract.
- Use the safe non-spoofable org-context RLS pattern. Do not rely on unsafe direct custom GUC `SET` by app users. Do not declare `LEAKPROOF` unless the implementation proves it is valid and necessary.
- Lower numeric priority is picked sooner; dependencies must be local `T-XXX` IDs. Cross-module blockers, if any, belong in `pipeline_inputs.cross_module_dependencies` and in this prompt.
- Exactly one atomic task type: `T1-schema`.

## Implementation contract
Add `_foundation/contracts/shared-bom-ssot.md` and optional JSON schema `_foundation/contracts/shared-bom-ssot.schema.json`. Define canonical entities and fields without implementing tables: `shared_bom`, `shared_bom_revision`, `shared_bom_line`, scope `org_id`, links to `fg_id`/finished_good, `factory_spec_id`, material identifiers, qty/uom, allergens carry-forward flags, effective dates, revision status (`draft`, `in_review`, `approved`, `superseded`), source module, D365 external IDs as optional fields only. State ownership: Foundation owns contract; Technical owns factory-use approval; NPD can create initial in_review draft; D365 never source of truth. Add validation examples and consumer responsibilities.

## Files in scope
- _foundation/contracts/shared-bom-ssot.md [create]
- _foundation/contracts/shared-bom-ssot.schema.json [create]

## Dependencies
- T-047
- T-048

## Cross-module dependencies
- 01-NPD creates initial shared BOM draft; 03-TECHNICAL approves factory-use BOM

## Acceptance criteria
1. Contract states shared BOM is SSOT and org-scoped via `org_id`.
2. Contract states D365 identifiers are optional external IDs and D365 is not source of truth.
3. Contract defines revision states including initial NPD Builder output `in_review`.
4. JSON schema parses and includes required top-level org_id, fg_id, revision, status, lines.

## Test strategy
- Run `python3 -m json.tool _foundation/contracts/shared-bom-ssot.schema.json`.
- Run grep checks for `org_id`, `in_review`, `D365`, `not source of truth`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not create DB migrations here.
- Do not assign Technical implementation details beyond the contract.
- Do not use tenant_id for BOM scope.

## Closeout evidence
Provide changed_files, test_commands_and_results, acceptance_criteria_status, deviations_from_prd, and git_status.
